---
title: "Research"
subtitle: "Active and recent projects in AI safety + biosecurity"
description: "Austin P. Morrissey's research projects in AI capability evaluations, biosecurity threat modeling, and adversarial elicitation."
date-modified: today
---

A snapshot of what I'm currently working on. For published / peer-reviewed work, see [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=uMwJNXEAAAAJ&hl=en).

## Active

### SEBE — Standardized Elicitation for Biosecurity Evals

A model-agnostic framework for separating *"the model won't say"* from *"the model doesn't know."* Tiered jailbreak sequences (T0–T4) implemented in Inspect AI, validated against the VCT benchmark.

> Phase 1 results: 84.6% breach rate against Sonnet 4.6 on 65 baseline-blocked questions; 31% extraction accuracy among breached. Implicit-extraction strategies (dialogue-based) decisively outperform explicit MCQ formatting.

[Source on GitHub](https://github.com/gvmfhy/sebe) (private at present)

### VCT Classifier Boundary Mapping

Comprehensive characterization of a frontier-model biosecurity classifier across five experiments (~1,750 probes + ~224 multi-turn conversations), yielding a three-gate hierarchical model and ten parameterized mutation rules.

> Final classifier model (v7.1) treats safety as vocabulary-token evaluation rather than semantic intent. Ten mutation rules show the boundary is lexical, not conceptual.

### Boundary Project

A reproduction of AISI's "Boundary" optimizer (arXiv:2602.15001v2) — evolutionary algorithm for jailbreak generation. Implemented 9-arm bandit with 56 seeds, structural crossover, span-edit operators, and template-fill from 15 FORTRESS-derived templates.

[Source on GitHub](https://github.com/gvmfhy/Boundary)

## Recent

### MIT Lincoln Laboratory & The Dual-Use Frontier

Substack essay on the structural challenges of dual-use research at federal labs. [Read on Substack](https://austinpatrick.substack.com/p/mit-lincoln-laboratory-dual-use-frontier).

### Evaluating LLM hypothesis generation in biology is hard

Substack essay on why current eval methodologies for scientific reasoning miss what actually matters in lab work. [Read on Substack](https://austinpatrick.substack.com/p/evaluating-llm-hypothesis-generation).

## Tools / open-source

- **[Inspect GUI](https://github.com/gvmfhy/inspect-gui)** — visual editor for Inspect AI evaluation pipelines
- **[c2-swarm](https://github.com/gvmfhy)** — four-layer military-style C2 architecture for adversarial probing of safety classifiers (information-quarantine via observation schemas)

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
For collaborations, see [the about page](/about.qmd).
:::
